channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: All Confirmed & Rumored Games For Summer Game Fest 2026
item.content:encoded:
The next rendition of Summer Game Fest is scheduled for June 5, and according to the host Geoff Keighley, it’s expected to be the ‘biggest live show yet.’

After such claims, it’s hard not to get excited, but the obvious question still remains: what games are going to feature during Summer Game Fest 2026? In the build-up to the event, some games are being confirmed to appear, whilst others are highly anticipated.

With that in mind, to help you prepare for the blockbuster event, we’ve collected a list of every confirmed and rumored game for Summer Game Fest 2026—check them all out below.

Every Confirmed Game For Summer Game Fest 2026

As things stand, six games have been confirmed to appear at Summer Game Fest, though that could change as we draw closer to the event.

  • More Insider Gaming: Summer Game Fest 2026 Showcase—Date & Start Times, How to Watch & How Long it is

Here’s every game that’s been confirmed to appear during Summer Game Fest 2026:

  • Star Wars Zero Company
  • Guild Wars
  • Fortnite
  • Blood Message
  • Among Us
  • Clutch

Fortnite’s new season will premiere at the event, whilst a world-first look at gameplay for Star Wars Zero Company will also be present. Maverick’s new driving game CLUTCH, as well as Blood Message, will receive a new trailer, but Among Us and Guild Wars’ involvement remains a bit of a mystery.

Every Rumored Game For Summer Game Fest 2026

At a showcase like Summer Game Fest, truly anything is possible. Every brand and publisher could show up, including PlayStation, which just held its own State of Play a few days ago, as well as XBOX, despite its own showcase being scheduled for June 7.

So, before we get into rumors and speculation, be sure to temper your expectations, whilst also anticipating surprise reveals—after all, this is the home of world premieres!

Without further ado, here’s what we think has a strong chance of appearing at Summer Game Fest 2026:

Final Fantasy 7 Part 3

This one is practically a given, as reliable insider Nate The Hate revealed that he expects the game to be featured at this year’s event. That said, to what capacity the game appears remains to be seen; after all, we don’t even know the game’s official name yet.

  • More Insider Gaming: Official Summer Game Fest 2026 Event Calendar—All Shows, Dates, Times & Schedule

Considering the director has already completed over 40 full playthroughs of the game, it would be fair to assume that development is progressing well. Could we get the full shebang: title, gameplay, and release date? Probably not, but time will surely tell.

Alien Isolation 2

It’s been a decade since Alien Isolation was released, and just a few months ago, it was confirmed that a sequel was officially in development. No title or release window was provided, and it was mentioned that more details would be shared when the team are ready. Is SGF too soon for it to show up? Maybe, but it’s one that fans are dying to see more of.

Intergalactic: The Heretic Prophet

I included this in the State of Play predictions, and it obviously didn’t show up, but that doesn’t mean it couldn’t still yet appear during Summer Game Fest. After all, it’s arguably one of the most highly anticipated games out there, and Sony will likely want some semblance of presence at the event—what better way to do so than with a new Intergalactic trailer?

Marvel 1943: Rise of Hydra

It feels like Marvel 1943: Rise of Hydra just can’t catch a break. It’s been delayed multiple times, but it’s currently slated for a late 2026 release window. With that in mind, this could finally be the time we see a concrete release date, alongside a brand new trailer.

Injustice 3/DC Fighting Game

It’s been nearly ten years since Injustice 2 hit shelves, and paired with Mortal Kombat, it’s been the bread and butter for NetherRealm Studios. It’s been claimed that another DC fighting game is in the works, though it remains unclear whether it’s a sequel to Injustice 2 or something new entirely: perhaps an official reveal is just around the corner.

Metro 2039

Alongside its exciting reveal back in April this year, it was confirmed that Metro 2039 has a release window of Winter 2026. Now would be the perfect time to reveal a new gameplay trailer to build on its initial hype, though it could also appear during the XBOX showcase.

Halo: Campaign Evolved

Another one that could also appear during the XBOX showcase is Halo: Campaign Evolved. That said, with the game also releasing on PlayStation 5, there’s a chance its release date is announced during Summer Game Fest, with some neutral ground a potential option.

Hogwarts Legacy 2

After the success of the original, it’s long been known that a sequel is in the works, with it being a priority for Warner Bros. Games. It wouldn’t be too big of a surprise if it were to appear during Summer Game Fest, though official confirmation would surely be welcome.

Some Big Exclusives/World Premieres

As I mentioned above, Summer Game Fest is often home to some of the biggest reveals in the gaming industry. It’s impossible to predict them all, especially if it’s the first time we’ve heard of a game! Whether it’s a brand new IP or an entry into a beloved franchise, expect some world premieres that nobody could have expected—Highguard 2, anyone?

Other Potential Appearances

That said, there’s plenty more out there that could appear during Summer Game Fest, so here’s a hefty list of other titles that could also receive some form of new announcement:

  • Resident Evil 9 DLC
  • Resident Evil Code Veronica Remake
  • Forza Horizon 6 PS5 Release Date
  • Castlevania Belmont’s Curse
  • Monster Hunter Wilds DLC
  • Light No Fire
  • Kena: Scars of Kosmora
  • Star Wars Jedi 3
  • Jurassic Park Survival
  • Lords of the Fallen 2
  • Beast of Reincarnation
  • Persona 6
  • Fallout 3 Remake
  • Netflix/Delphi FIFA World Cup

Are you excited for Summer Game Fest 2026? Do you think we’re missing any potential reveals? If so, let us know in the comments below, or share your pre-show predictions in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more Insider Gaming content, check out the following:

  • Insider Gaming Showcase—Everything Announced
  • Xbox Games Showcase 2026 & Gears of War: E-Day Direct—Date, Start Times & How to Watch
  • Best Sony PlayStation Days of Play 2026 Deals: End Date & Sale Games

Charlie is one of Insider Gaming's Guides Writers who's been in the gaming industry for a little over a year. Unfortunately for him, he enjoys every video game genre out…

More from Charlie Champion

MOBILE APP

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: FF7 Remake Part 3 Could Be Revealed at Summer Game Fest
item.content:encoded:
According to a reliable insider, Final Fantasy 7 Remake Part 3 is expected at the Summer Game Fest 2026.

Summer Game Fest 2026 is all set to kick off on Friday, June 5, 2026, and will feature a whole bunch of announcements, as Geoff Keighley has revealed it will be the biggest SGF live show yet. It seems that players might see the final addition to the Final Fantasy 7 Remake series appear at the event as well.

Final Fantasy 7 Remake Part 3 Expected at Summer Game Fest 2026

In a thread on r/GamingLeaksAndRumours, insider Nate the Hate was asked if he knew whether Final Fantasy 7 Remake Part 3 would be featured at this year’s Summer Game Fest. He responded that he expects it to be included in the event. When asked whether this was merely a prediction or something he had explicitly heard, Nate clarified that he had “heard” that Final Fantasy 7 Remake Part 3 will be at the Summer Game Fest.

We have yet to receive a title for FF7 Remake Part 3, leaving players eagerly awaiting any new information from Square Enix. It appears that development is well underway, as the director has already completed over 40 full playthroughs of the game. This suggests that only the polishing bit might be left. Additionally, this installment will be the first in the series to release on all platforms simultaneously, which has significantly raised expectations.

In other news, Final Fantasy 7 Remake Part 3’s director has confirmed that they have left the door open for DLC. Additionally, the upcoming title will feature new gameplay mechanics. What are your thoughts on Final Fantasy 7 Remake Part 3 being revealed at Summer Game Fest? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Star Wars Zero Company’s release date, price, and other details. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

item.title: G2 Esports Announce English Release of Red Aura Comic on WEBTOON
item.content:encoded:
G2 Esports has announced that its original web comic series ‘Red Aura’ is about to receive its English-language release.

The fantasy-action series will release on popular digital comic platform WEBTOON on Saturday, June 6, under the WEBTOON Originals label.

G2 revealed the new IP in October last year as part of the organisation’s 10th anniversary.

The comic originally debuted in Japan through WEBTOON’s LINE MANGA platform in October 2025, where it reached #9 in New Series on its opening weekend.

Red Aura will now launch on the English-language WEBTOON site with 20 episodes available immediately, including 15 free episodes and 5 Fast Pass chapters.

More episodes will be released weekly, with a planned 42-episode first season.

To celebrate the launch, G2 creator and VALORANT streamer Ryan ‘Shanks’ Ngo will host a live reading of Red Aura.

What is G2’s Red Aura?

Red Aura is inspired by the “defining moments and competitive spirit” of G2 and reimagines the organisation’s history through shonen-inspired storytelling.

The plot sees the story set in a world of magical ‘Nomads,’ characters with incredible powers, with main character, Asa, a young girl who dreams of becoming the greatest Nomad who ever lived.

In a fantasy-action world shaped by rivalry, perseverance, and competition, Asa has to learn to master her abilities while facing dangerous enemies and the brutal consequences of ambition.

Red Aura was created in collaboration with KISAI Entertainment, the studio behind the webcomic adaptations of AVATAR: The Last Airbender, alongside Japanese Art Director Runbel, who works on the previously #1 action fantasy series in Japan and Korea – Skill Master Levels Up.

The press release from G2 claims the organisation hope to continue investing in anime-inspired entertainment and original storytelling designed for digital-first audiences, having also previously partnered with Solo Levelling for a merchandise release.

What other forms of media would you like to see esports organisations explore? Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord.

Additionally, G2 Esports partnered with McDonald’s Germany for its G2 NORD and G2 Hel LoL teams.

MOBILE APP

item.title: Star Wars Zero Company Release Date Revealed
item.content:encoded:
Information from a reliable insider reveals that the Star Wars Zero Company release date has been set for August 2026.

Players were recently informed that Star Wars Zero Company’s gameplay will be showcased on Friday as part of Summer Game Fest 2026, with many new details to be revealed at the event. However, the latest report provides players with the release date, price, and early access information.

Star Wars Zero Company August 2026 Release Date Revealed with Pricing, Early Access Information, and More

A new report by billbil-kun on Dealabs reveals the release date for Star Wars: Zero Company, along with much more information. According to their investigations, the release date for the upcoming Star Wars title has been set for August 27, 2026, and it will be released on PS5, Xbox Series X/S, and PC that day; Switch 2 wasn’t mentioned.

Regarding pricing, it looks like the price will differ between the PC and console versions of Star Wars Zero Company. On PC, the Standard edition is priced at $49.99, whereas on console it’s slightly higher at $59.99. The situation is similar for Deluxe Edition, with the PC version costing $59.99 and the console version priced at $69.99. The contents of the Deluxe edition are unknown, but the report has confirmed there will be no early access.

There is a physical release planned for the Standard Edition of Star Wars Zero Company; Deluxe is only available as a Deluxe edition. However, there is an option to upgrade to the Deluxe edition as well.

Talking about Star Wars Zero Company, the upcoming title received some new screenshots earlier this year. In other news, Lucasfilm has revealed that players are going to receive a lot more stories for Star Wars Jedi: The Fallen Order’s Cal Kestis. What are your thoughts on Star Wars Zero Company releasing in August 2026? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about God of War Laufey ‘definitely’ releasing before 2028. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

item.title: Escape from Tarkov Update Introduces Hugely Needed Change
item.content:encoded:
Battlestate Games has pushed out a new Escape from Tarkov update that introduces a sorely needed change, directly related to the new Icebreaker map transition. This is in response to the community crying out about ‘transition campers’ making their lives a living hell on Shoreline.

It’s only a minor update, but it’s a quality-of-life thing that will have a major impact, with Battlestate praising it as the fastest time between people asking for it and them delivering it.

Icebreaker Transition Has Been Moved

In a post on social media, Battlestate Games wrote:

The transit to Icebreaker location has been moved closer to a beginning of pier by blue fence to reduce the number of cases of transit camping.

For the uninitiated, accessing the new Icebreaker map is only possible via a transition, which costs 400,00 rubles and takes place at the pier on Shoreline. In recent days, we’ve seen some taking great pleasure in sitting back from a long sightline and watching for players marching up the pier to the waiting transit point.

Icebreaker was a frustrating update for a few reasons, but this was one of the soul-crushing drawbacks of the new map.

Tarkov’s design manager, Sergei Domontovich, explained it was a ‘world record’ for the shortest time between a promise being made and a change being delivered.

Does this Escape from Tarkov update fill you with excitement? Let us know what you’re thinking on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that God of War Laufey should arrive before 2028

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

item.title: God of War Laufey Release Will ‘Definitely’ Be Before 2028
item.content:encoded:
According to a reputable insider, players will not have to wait several years for the release of God of War: Laufey.

In case you missed the latest PlayStation State of Play, Santa Monica Studios announced a brand-new God of War title, which has Kratos’ wife, Faye, front and center. The announcement was also not just a teaser or a title reveal; players got to see over 20 minutes of gameplay of what looked like the first mission. However, no release date or window was mentioned.

God of War Laufey Release ‘Isn’t Years Away’

A recent post on Jason Schreier’s BlueSky account discusses God of War: Laufey not receiving a release window at the State of Play reveal. Schreier’s post asks players not to overthink the upcoming title’s release date or window, as compared to “a lot of other big announcements, this one *isn’t* years away.” In the comments on this post, Schreier was asked whether God of War: Laufey would be released in 2028, to which the insider replied, “Definitely not.”

God of War: Laufey is the next main installment in the God of War series developed by Santa Monica. The story follows Faye’s journey, beginning immediately after her funeral, as she finds herself in the gods’ afterlife. In a realm known as Everywhen, she confronts gods and creatures from various mythologies while seeking a way to escape.

Faye, known as the Golden Hand of Jotnar, possesses such power that even Thor regards her as an equal. She can withstand hits as heavily as Kratos, leading the developers to design her combat style to emphasize lethality, power, and fluidity, as showcased in the new trailer.

In other news, God of War Laufey has brought back a feature that hasn’t been seen in the past 13 years. Additionally, an actor from ABC’s Lost will play Freya in the upcoming live-action adaptation of God of War. What are your thoughts on God of War: Laufey’s release coming before 2028? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about WWE’s Rhea Ripley naming PlayStation classics as her favorite games. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.
